candidates.target: MPI communication synchronization overhead (excessive blocking + barriers)
candidates.rationale: Profiling shows MPI_Waitall at 40% and message exchange dominating runtime, indicating heavy time spent blocked in communication. The retrieved 'Non-Blocking I/O' pattern targets reducing time spent blocked/waiting and improving latency/CPU efficiency under synchronization-heavy behavior, aligning best with the observed mpi_wait_pct=40.
candidates.action_sketch: Replace the per-chunk blocking MPI_Send/MPI_Recv + surrounding MPI_Barrier calls with non-blocking MPI_Isend/MPI_Irecv and a single MPI_Waitall per iteration (or per small batch of chunks), preserving the same peers/tags/ordering by posting receives and sends for the same tags and waiting for completion before reusing/modifying buffer regions; remove intra-chunk barriers and keep at most one barrier for timing if needed.
preconditions: Send/recv buffers (buf+offset regions) are not modified or reused until the corresponding non-blocking request completes
Both ranks post matching Irecv/Isend with identical tags (1000+c and 2000+c) and communicator (MPI_COMM_WORLD)
correctness_checks: Verify checksum remains identical to baseline for same (msg_size,iters,chunks)
Run with MPI message matching checks (same source/dest, tags 1000+c and 2000+c) and confirm no deadlock across all iterations
Enable MPI error checking (return codes) and run under an MPI correctness tool (e.g., with debug build) to ensure requests complete before buffer reuse
rollback_criteria: Any checksum mismatch or MPI error/deadlock
mpi_wait_pct does not decrease or total TIME_SEC regresses by >5% versus baseline
